hypothesis: Among the Si–X alloy candidates identified by the AI framework as exhibiting invariant Li⁺ migration channel dimensions across lithiation states, those incorporating alloying elements with higher bulk moduli than silicon (e.g., W, Mo, or Ti) will demonstrate a statistically significant inverse correlation between the alloying element's bulk modulus and the slope of channel radius versus state-of-charge, such that substituting even a small fraction (≤12.5 at%) of these stiff elements into the Si sublattice will reduce volumetric expansion below 50% of pure Si while maintaining gravimetric capacity above 1500 mAh/g, a relationship that can be validated by DFT-computed elastic tensors and CHGNet-relaxed delithiation trajectories across a systematic compositional grid of Si₁₋ₓMₓ structures.
